proposals.title: Reframe severity from forecast-based to customer experience metrics
proposals.proposed_text: per-channel customer experience thresholds (ASA for chat/phone, average queue time for email)
proposals.rationale: Approved (R1). Severity scope and thresholds changed from forecast accuracy (5-10%) to customer experience metrics. Priority: 1) Chat (ASA), 2) Phone (ASA), 3) Email (avg time in non-handled queue).

proposals.title: Reframe severity: per-channel thresholds based on customer experience
proposals.proposed_text:
Severity is triggered per channel when customer experience metrics breach a threshold:
- Chat and phone: average speed of answer (ASA) - time from queue entry to first agent response
- Email: average time in non-handled queue
Priority ranking for lever activation: 1) Chat, 2) Phone, 3) Email.
When any channel breaches its threshold, severity moves from Normal to Elevated or Critical, triggering the corresponding levers. Not all levers help all channels - downstream sections clarify which levers apply where.
proposals.rationale: Rejected-and-revised (R1). Original proposal was email-first framing. Roman's correction: severity = per-channel customer experience metrics (ASA, queue time). Any channel breaching threshold triggers severity change. Need to clarify which levers help which channels.

proposals.title: SLA thresholds: per-channel, email is health indicator
proposals.current_text: Is an aggregate SLA across all channels sufficient, or do different channels need different escalation triggers?
proposals.proposed_text: Decided: Per-channel thresholds. Each channel has its own metric (ASA for chat/phone, queue time for email). Email backlog is the overall health indicator but all channels are monitored independently.
proposals.rationale: Approved (R1). Updated to align with the per-channel customer experience metric framing.

proposals.title: Move variance baseline to operational visibility section
proposals.proposed_text: Variance baseline: BPOs carry a contractual buffer of +/- 10% on contact forecast. This buffer is often consumed by absenteeism, so effective headroom may be lower. Dashboard should surface this gap.
proposals.rationale: Rejected-and-moved (R1). Roman: this belongs in operational visibility as dashboard content, not in severity stages. The 10% buffer and absenteeism caveat should be visible on the dashboard.

proposals.title: QAs/trainers: Critical only
proposals.current_text: team leads, QAs, trainers, support staff take contacts
proposals.proposed_text: team leads, QAs, trainers, support staff take contacts (Critical only)
proposals.rationale: Approved (R1). Roman: no need for Elevated nuance context. Just list the lever with its severity trigger.

proposals.title: OT ownership: WFM owns end-to-end (decided)
proposals.current_text: OX coordinates if multiple sites involved
proposals.proposed_text: WFM identifies need, collects from BPOs, and tracks OT production
proposals.rationale: Decided (R1). WFM owns OT end-to-end: identifies need, collects info from BPOs, tracks production. Rationale: if WFM sees the need and OX collects, WFM still has to track - same workload. RACI must also be updated to reflect this.

proposals.title: Remove language/channel attribution as lever
proposals.current_text: Language/channel attribution to isolate the driver
proposals.proposed_text: [Remove this lever row - attribution is visibility/reporting, not an operational lever]
proposals.rationale: Rejected-and-simplified (R1). Roman: don't annotate what was moved. Just remove it as a lever. Attribution belongs in visibility/reporting.

proposals.title: Add appendix: discussed but not included items
proposals.proposed_text:
Appendix: Items discussed but not included in current framework
| Item | Discussion summary | Reason for exclusion |
| Break rescheduling | Operationally complex, may lead to break accumulation | BPO-autonomous decision, not centrally directed |
| [Additional items from rejected proposals to be added here] |
proposals.rationale: New proposal (R1). Roman: for everything rejected, create an appendix table of items discussed but not included in the current framework.
